generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LidarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for 3D environmental mapping and object detection in autonomous vehicle applications."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LidarPointCloudData
]

instance DepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera providing short-range perception and proximity tracking for autonomous vehicle obstacle detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Telemetry sensors capturing real-time vehicle dynamics including motor state, steering angles, servo actuation, and overall vehicle state."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor actuation system receiving control commands for vehicle propulsion and speed regulation."
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering actuation system receiving control commands for vehicle direction adjustment."
]

instance ServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo actuation system for fine control of vehicle components in autonomous driving applications."
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LidarPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from LiDAR sensor to Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking component via Wi-Fi UDP at millisecond-level intervals."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LidarSensor
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Depth camera data transmitted for short-range perception and proximity tracking via Wi-Fi UDP."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle telemetry including motor state, steering angles, servo actuation, and vehicle dynamics transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed actuation feedback data from motor, steering, and servo systems transmitted to Digital Twin Entity for validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuator, SteeringActuator, ServoActuator
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and driving paths generated by Adaptive Cruise Control system for visualization and monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringAndTracking
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model representing 3D environmental mapping from LiDAR point cloud data for object detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTracking, DataTranslationFE
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model capturing real-time vehicle dynamics and state for control and monitoring purposes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model for tracking detected objects and generating driving paths for Adaptive Cruise Control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTracking, DataMonitoringService
]

instance ProcessedLidarData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered and clustered LiDAR point cloud data converted into structured object representations for visualization and control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LidarPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTracking, DataTranslationFE
]

instance ProcessedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized and validated vehicle state data including motor, steering, and servo actuation commands."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and driving paths generated from raw LiDAR data for visualization and decision support."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringService, DataStorage
]

instance ActuationCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuation commands derived from Adaptive Cruise Control logic for vehicle control execution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

// C11: Enablers
instance Ros2Network : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) network facilitating real-time communication between software components within Digital Twin Entity via topic-based messaging."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringAndTracking, DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE, DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance NvidiaJetsonNx : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson NX microcomputer providing onboard autonomy processing capabilities for sensor fusion and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringAndTracking, DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE
]

instance NvidiaJetsonOrinNano : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer offering enhanced AI/ML acceleration for real-time autonomy processing."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringAndTracking, DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE, DataMonitoringService
]

instance NvidiaJetpackSdk : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack SDK enabling AI/ML acceleration and robotics development on Jetson platforms."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringAndTracking, DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE
]

instance WifiUdpProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi UDP protocol enabling high-speed, low-latency communication between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity for real-time data transmission."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity
]

// C6: Services
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTracking : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Software component performing real-time LiDAR point cloud clustering and object tracking to generate structured object representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessedLidarInsight, ObjectTrackingAction
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Functional Entity ensuring data serialization, unit conversion, and message type alignment between disparate data sources and ROS2 network."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTranslationInsight, DataValidationInsight
]

instance ActuationFE : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Functional Entity processing and validating actuation commands for motor, steering, and servo systems based on ACC logic."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationValidationInsight, SafetyCommandAction
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data visualization service displaying vehicle state, object tracks, and driving paths for operators and developers."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight, HistoricalReportingInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Distributed computation service handling ACC logic execution, path planning, and sensor fusion offloaded from resource-constrained onboard ECUs."
    DTDFVocab:provides AccControlInsight, PathPlanningAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Teleoperation service enabling remote ACC control, configuration, and emergency intervention for autonomous vehicle management."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ProcessedLidarInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Human-interpretable object representations derived from raw LiDAR point cloud data through clustering and tracking algorithms."
]

instance ObjectTrackingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation and maintenance of object tracks and driving paths for Adaptive Cruise Control system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTranslationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Consistent data format alignment across all sensor inputs and system components through serialization and unit conversion."
]

instance DataValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Cross-validation of processed data against real-time vehicle state to ensure data consistency and reliability."
]

instance ActuationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of actuation commands to prevent unsafe motor, steering, or servo operations based on ACC logic."
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Low-latency visualization of vehicle state, object tracks, and driving paths for real-time monitoring and decision support."
]

instance HistoricalReportingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical data persistence and reporting capabilities for post-mission analysis and system validation."
]

instance AccControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive Adaptive Cruise Control logic execution including lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and safety checks."
]

instance PathPlanningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of optimal driving paths based on real-time environmental perception and vehicle state."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Remote management capabilities including ACC enable/disable, desired speed setting, and system configuration."
]

instance SafetyCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of emergency braking commands based on safety-critical conditions detected by ACC system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual or automatic emergency braking command execution with highest priority for safety-critical situations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components (already covered above)

// C10: Additional Models/Data
instance AccLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model implementing Adaptive Cruise Control algorithms for lead vehicle detection, speed regulation, and safety checks."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingService, ActuationFE
]

instance VisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D visualization model converting processed data into human-interpretable graphical representations for monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringService
]

// C11: Additional Enablers
instance CustomDevelopedUI : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom-developed user interface providing real-time data visualization, logging, and remote management capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data storage component for persistence of processed vehicle state, object tracks, and historical data for reporting."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalReportingInsight
]

// C6: Additional Services
instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Entity handling bidirectional data exchange between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity via Wi-Fi UDP protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataCollectionInsight, DeviceControlAction
]

// C17: Additional Insights/Actions
instance DataCollectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of OME states through bidirectional data exchange with Physical Twin Entity."
]

instance DeviceControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic control of physical vehicle components based on Digital Twin Entity commands."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the transmission of control commands from the **Digital Twin Entity** (e.g., user inputs, ACC logic) to the **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., motor actuation, steering servo). The **Control Panel User Interface FE** allows users to send remote management commands, while the **ACC Controlling FE** generates automated control signals (e.g., desired speed adjustments). These commands are translated by the **Data Translation FE**, which serializes and formats them into executable messages. The **Actuation FE** then converts these commands into physical actions, such as **motor voltage commands** for speed control or **steering servo adjustments**. Communication between the Digital Twin and Physical Twin entities occurs over a **Wi-Fi network using UDP**, while internal software components use **ROS2**. No explicit validation, authentication, or conflict resolution mechanisms are described in the documents, though the **Data Translation FE** ensures message alignment and unit consistency. Feedback systems are implied through the **Data Monitoring** service, which visualizes real-time vehicle state and object tracks, but no explicit confirmation mechanisms (e.g., acknowledgments) are documented."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** across all devices to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This synchronization is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter standards (e.g., <100 ms) are mandated. Additionally, the system must maintain a **network latency of at most 100 ms** from the vehicle to the Digital Twin Entity (DT Entity), ensuring real-time responsiveness for time-sensitive operations. The temporal granularity is implicitly constrained by the ROS2-based communication framework, where message passing and service calls must align with these latency and synchronization requirements to preserve system integrity. No explicit sub-millisecond or microsecond-level synchronization is documented beyond the 100 ms threshold."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **ISO 23247 RA-inspired, entity-centric orchestration pattern** with the following key characteristics:
  - **Component Integration**: Software components are mapped to **Functional Entities (FEs)** and implemented as **ROS2 packages**, where each package encapsulates one or more ROS2 nodes. This enables **modular, service-oriented integration** with clear boundaries between entities (*Physical Twin*, *Digital Twin*, *User*).
  - **Resource Management**: Hardware resources (compute, sensors, actuators) are explicitly tied to the *Physical Twin Entity*, which hosts both **resource-specific** (e.g., sensor data ingestion) and **general automotive** functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering*). This design **centralizes resource ownership** within the entity but requires careful engineering to manage cross-entity dependencies (e.g., data flow from sensors to visualization).
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: The architecture leverages **ROS2’s distributed computing model**, where nodes communicate via topics/services across entities. The *Digital Twin Entity* acts as a **mediator** for data management and control, while the *User Entity* provides human-machine interfaces. Deviations from ISO 23247 (e.g., *Physical Twin Entity* vs. OME) introduce **potential coordination challenges**, particularly in ownership of functionalities like *Point Cloud Clustering*, which may require cross-entity synchronization. No explicit orchestrator (e.g., Kubernetes, custom middleware) is mentioned, implying reliance on ROS2’s native mechanisms."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) relies on a **hybrid communication architecture** combining **ROS2** for intra-entity software component communication and **Wi-Fi with UDP transport** for inter-entity communication (e.g., between Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User Entities). The system specifies a **network latency requirement of at most 100 ms** for inter-entity communication, ensuring real-time synchronization between the physical and digital domains. The **Data Translation FE** is central to this infrastructure, as it serializes data, aligns message types, and performs unit conversions for all data streams. No explicit details on bandwidth requirements, redundancy mechanisms (e.g., redundant publishers), or security protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication) are provided in the documents. The architecture is designed to support low-latency, high-reliability data exchange critical for autonomous vehicle control and monitoring."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin System (DTS) Hosting and Deployment** architecture is designed for **distributed execution**, with each entity (Physical, Digital, User) deployed on separate hardware components:

  **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **Physical Twin Entity (Vehicle)**: Deployed on embedded hardware (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson NX/Nano) for real-time sensor processing and actuation.
  - **Digital Twin Entity (Virtual Cockpit)**: Deployed **externally** (offloaded from the vehicle), leveraging a **separate hosting platform** (not specified in the document, but implied to be a dedicated server or edge device).
  - **User Entity (Control Panel)**: Deployed on a **user-facing interface hardware** (e.g., laptop, tablet, or dedicated HMI).

  **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
  - **Physical Twin**: Requires low-latency processing for sensor fusion (LiDAR, motor/steering data) and actuation commands.
  - **Digital Twin**: Relies on **3D Visualization** and **Reporting FE** components, which may require higher computational resources for rendering and data analytics.
  - **Data Storage**: Implicit use of a **SQL database** (mentioned in the context of Reporting FE) for structured data storage (e.g., logs, reports).

  **Scalability Mechanisms**: Not explicitly described in the document.
  **Deployment Models Supported**:
  - **Hybrid Deployment**: Combines embedded (Physical Twin), external (Digital Twin), and user-facing (Control Panel) components.
  - **Modular Design**: Each functional element (FE) is deployable independently, enabling flexibility in scaling or updating components.

  **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  - **Redundancy**: Not explicitly described, but implied by the separation of entities (e.g., Digital Twin can operate independently of the Physical Twin for simulation/testing).
  - **Fault Tolerance**: Not explicitly described in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard (2021)**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - An **entity-based framework** with standardized roles (**OME, Data Collection Entity, DTE, User Entity**).
  - **Interoperability** via modular **Functional Entities (FEs)**, allowing integration with protocols like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** (though not explicitly mandated).
  - **Compliance frameworks** are implied through the standard’s **four-part structure** (Overview, Reference Architecture, Digital Representation, Information Exchange), ensuring alignment with **manufacturing-specific digital twin requirements**.
  Certification requirements or third-party validation processes are **not detailed** in the provided documents."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) and V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services adheres to **ISO 15622:2018** for performance requirements and test procedures, ensuring functional safety and reliability in dynamic driving conditions. **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 (Release 15)** specifies service requirements for V2X, including latency constraints (e.g., <100 ms for critical safety messages) and communication reliability. While the system is designed for **safety-critical applications**, the provided documents do not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, PKI, or intrusion detection) or **access control mechanisms**. The absence of **ISO 26262-1:2018** or explicit threat mitigation strategies in the references suggests a focus on **functional safety** (e.g., fail-safe mechanisms for ACC) rather than comprehensive cybersecurity frameworks. Risk assessment is implied through standardized performance testing (ISO 15622) but lacks granularity on **authentication systems** or **data integrity measures**."
]
